what_it_does: Summarizes existing graph-redesign evidence, mapping observed data shapes, reusable UI primitives, palette/icon resources, design laws, completed work, and unresolved Tim decisions into reuse/extend/do-not-duplicate/constraining implications.
observations: States the file is intended as pre-work ground truth for builders before changing the graph redesign.
Organizes findings into sections for projected graph data, reusable graph primitives, palette/icons, design laws, completed work, and unresolved forks.
Uses tables with Finding, Where, and Implication columns for evidence-backed claims.
Records many implications explicitly as REUSE, EXTEND, DON'T-DUPLICATE, or CONSTRAINS.
Identifies current graph data fields and absent fields such as depth, order, and mtime in the artifact.
Separates recoverable rerun-derived data from non-durable live filesystem time.
Emphasizes augmenting existing graph rendering and interaction code rather than building a parallel graph generator.
Captures open Tim decisions as numbered G1 through G10.
States that several prior premises are false or settled, including the idea that edges only draw on tap.
standouts: The file treats line-referenced evidence as the driver for design constraints and implementation scope.
It distinguishes implementation-ready implications from unresolved product/design forks.
It repeatedly guards against duplicate graph systems, duplicate V-brand rendering, and premature closure of open semantics.
conventions: Uses uppercase implication labels to classify each finding.
Keeps source locations in a Where column with file paths and line references where available.
Uses explicit OPEN→Tim markers for unresolved decisions.
Uses dense prose with parenthetical identifiers such as F1, E1, PF-6, and G4.
Mixes verified observations, implementation implications, and decision-gate notes in the same table rows.
concerns: The document is dense and assumes familiarity with many local identifiers such as PF-6, R12-R15, F1, and E1.
Some rows contain multiple claims and implications, making individual facts harder to isolate.
The stated no-cross-file verification posture depends on many referenced files that are not included in this file itself.
notes: Briefing note: this is an evidence ledger for the graph redesign that tells a builder what to reuse, extend, avoid duplicating, and leave open for Tim.
questions: What document defines the shorthand identifiers such as F1, E1, M1, PF-6, and R12-R15?
Which open Tim decisions have since been answered, if any?
Where should the type hue map ultimately live if G1 is resolved?
How should this synthesis be updated when the graph artifact is regenerated?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it provides a pre-implementation synthesis of existing evidence, constraints, reuse points, and unresolved forks for the graph redesign.
intention_signals: It seems intended to prevent redesign drift by anchoring graph work in existing data, primitives, palette rules, interaction behavior, and named unresolved decisions.
novelty: A Markdown research synthesis that combines source-line evidence, design-law constraints, implementation implications, and decision-gate tracking in one ledger-style document.
contribution: Preserves a structured ledger of graph-redesign substrates: current data shape, reusable renderer and interaction surfaces, palette/icon resources, design constraints, completed scope, and unresolved decision points that a fused system would retain as traceable context.
summary_for_embedding: A graph-redesign research synthesis documenting existing substrate data, reusable UI graph primitives, palette and icon resources, governing design laws, completed work, and unresolved Tim decisions with file-path evidence and implications.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for orienting builders before graph redesign work so changes reuse existing substrates, respect constraints, avoid duplicate systems, and surface decisions that still need Tim.
